title: Odesa tragedy : Wars taking their toll on Indian seafarers
description: DAYS after India announced a real-time operational dashboard for tracking Indian seafarers, four of them were killed when a merchant vessel was hit by Russian cruise missiles shortly after it left the Ukrainian port of Odesa. The MV Golden Leo, which was sailing under the Guinea-Bissau flag and carrying Ukrainian corn, had Indians and Syrians among its crew members. As the war in eastern Europe intensifies, the tragedy has highlighted the growing dangers faced by civilian maritime workers who continue to sustain international trade amid armed hostilities. Regardless of the military objectives of the warring sides, attacks on commercial ships with civilian crews threaten the freedom of navigation that underpins the global economy.
content: DAYS after India announced a real-time operational dashboard for tracking Indian seafarers, four of them were killed when a merchant vessel was hit by Russian cruise missiles shortly after it left the Ukrainian port of Odesa. The MV Golden Leo, which... [1646 chars]

title: India ran a $4-trillion economy half-blind for decades. That just ended
description: For decades, India has tracked factory, mining, and electricity output every month through the Index of Industrial Production, even though services account for more than half of the economy. That gap has finally begun to close. The government has released monthly production indices for 19 service industries, using GST returns and regulatory data to estimate changes in real output. This could transform GDP forecasting, monetary-policy analysis, and investment research. But it is still only a trial series.

title: Global Markets: London Stock Exchange to launch round-the-clock trading next year
description: Starting in early 2027, the London Stock Exchange is set to unveil LSE 24, a pioneering venue designed for overnight trading of exchange-traded products. This initiative is a direct response to the increasing demand from international investors for expanded trading options. Although equities may be introduced on LSE 24 in the future, the main market will maintain its traditional hours.
content:

title: Rupee hits two-month low, oil prices remain focal point
description: The Indian rupee hit a two-month low on Monday as oil prices hovered around ninety dollars per barrel. In an effort to stabilize the currency, the Reserve Bank of India intervened by unloading dollars into the market. Meanwhile, Asian currencies and stock performances were varied, with the dollar index holding steady. This rise in energy prices could have implications for the Federal Reserve's future interest rate strategies.

title: How rural India is bearing the brunt of the inflation rise
description: India’s rural inflation has moved closer to 5%, while the urban rate remains below 4%. The gap is usually explained by higher food prices, but the latest data suggest price pressures may be spreading beyond food.
content: Rural inflation typically accelerates when food prices are rising or remain elevated. Food and beverages account for 23.2% of the rural basket, compared with 13.5% of the urban basket. With an uneven monsoon and the risk of deficient rainfall raising... [5377 chars]

description: Elevated inflation persists due to West Asian tensions and a weak monsoon outlook. The Reserve Bank of India maintains its policy rate, diverging from global peers. Governor Malhotra stated that domestic inflation pass-through has remained limited so far. India's economic fundamentals remain strong despite geopolitical uncertainties and growth forecasts. Policymakers will monitor inflationary pressures before considering any monetary policy adjustments.

title: Can banks increase FD interest rates as inflation crosses RBI’s target of 4%?
description: Rising inflation has crossed the Reserve Bank of India's target, signaling potential repo rate hikes. High yields on government securities and small savings schemes also pressure banks to increase FD rates.

title: India bonds extend recovery tracking rise in U.S. Treasuries
description: Indian government bonds saw gains for a second straight session on Thursday. This followed overnight increases in U.S. Treasuries after softer economic data emerged. State-run lenders and foreign investors actively bought bonds, adding support.
